It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Charcoal stopped moving after 3 clicks (now at cols 22-25). Bottom blue clicks no longer move it. Green gap at cols 28-29. Need to find another way to move charcoal.
- Goal model: Try other blue blocks to move charcoal block further right.
- Plan: Try clicking top blue block to see if it moves charcoal.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The top blue block click also didn't move the charcoal block. It's still at cols 22-25. The only change was `total_cells_changed: 1`, which is likely just the HUD.
So neither the bottom blue block nor the top blue block moves the charcoal block anymore. Let me try the middle blue blocks.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the problem. In level 1, I clicked the bottom blue block 5 times to move the charcoal block from cols 58-63 to cols 38-41 (20 columns left). Then I clicked it one more time to complete the level.
In level 2, I clicked the bottom blue block 6 times, and the charcoal block moved from cols 10-13 to cols 22-25 (12 columns right). The charcoal block stopped moving after 3 clicks.
Maybe the bottom blue block in level 2 only has 3 states, and after reaching the last state, further clicks don't change the state.
But then how do I complete the level? In level 1, I clicked the bottom blue block one more time after reaching the aligned state to complete the level. But in level 2, the charcoal block doesn't move anymore, so clicking the bottom blue block again doesn't complete the level.
Maybe I need to click a different blue block to complete the level. Or maybe I need to click the charcoal block or the green gap.
Let me try clicking the upper-middle blue block (rows 24-27) to see if it does something.
Actually, let me first check the gray hash to see if the state changed when I clicked the top blue block. If the gray hash changed, then the state changed, but the charcoal block didn't move. This would suggest that the state machine has more states, but the charcoal block only moves in some of them.
Let me check the gray hash.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Top blue click also didn't move charcoal (still at cols 22-25). Need to try middle blue blocks.
Goal model: Try upper-middle and lower-middle blue blocks to see if they move charcoal or complete the level.
Plan: Try clicking upper-middle blue block (rows 24-27).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check gray hash to track state
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
gray = [n for n in curr_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G'][0]
print(f"Current gray hash: {gray['hash']}")

# Try upper-middle blue block (rows 24-27)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 2}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)

curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
gray = [n for n in curr_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G'][0]
print(f"After click gray hash: {gray['hash']}")

for n in curr_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 1:
        print(f"Charcoal: boundary={n['boundary']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
